posts.title: New Year Water Management Message to the Government and People of Sierra Leone
posts.bodyHtml: New Year message from the National Water Resources Management Agency on water management priorities for Sierra Leone.

posts.title: MERRY CHRISTMAS AND HAPPY HOLIDAYS
posts.bodyHtml: Season's greetings from the National Water Resources Management Agency.

posts.title: Parliament Approves the New Director General for NWRMA
posts.excerpt: The new Director General, Ing. Pierre Palmer has been approved by Parliament as the successor to the outgoing Director General,
posts.bodyHtml: The new Director General, Ing. Pierre Palmer has been approved by Parliament as the successor to the outgoing Director General.

posts.title: IWMI Concludes Scoping Mission to Sierra Leone
posts.excerpt: Freetown, Sierra Leone – June 10, 2025 – A high-level scoping mission from the International Water Management Institute (IWMI) has concluded
posts.bodyHtml: Freetown, Sierra Leone – June 10, 2025 – A high-level scoping mission from the International Water Management Institute (IWMI) has concluded its work in Sierra Leone.

posts.title: Gov't of Sierra Leone Boosts Groundwater Knowledge among WASH Experts
posts.excerpt: The Government of Sierra Leone, through the World Bank-funded Food Systems Resilience Programme, has strengthened groundwater knowledge among WASH experts.
posts.bodyHtml: The Government of Sierra Leone, through the World Bank-funded Food Systems Resilience Programme, has strengthened groundwater knowledge among WASH experts.

posts.title: Declining Water Levels In Kono- A District Thirsting for Solutions
posts.excerpt: Kono District, historically known for its diamond mines and fertile lands, now grapples with an existential threat from declining water levels.
posts.bodyHtml: Kono District, historically known for its diamond mines and fertile lands, now grapples with an existential threat from declining water levels.
